differentials.name: 80% clinical accuracy (78-92% in males, 58-85% in females):
differentials.raw: 80% clinical accuracy (78-92% in males, 58-85% in females):

differentials.name: mild poorly localized visceral pain of 4-6 hours duration referred to epigastrium + periumbilical region
differentials.raw: mild poorly localized visceral pain of 4-6 hours duration referred to epigastrium + periumbilical region

differentials.name: crampy pain migrates into RLQ pain over appendix = McBurney sign
differentials.raw: crampy pain migrates into RLQ pain over appendix = McBurney sign (72%) and becomes continuous + more severe (somatic pain)

differentials.name: afebrile / low-grade fever (56%) ◊  Suspect perforation with temperature > 38.3°C
differentials.raw: afebrile / low-grade fever (56%) ◊  Suspect perforation with temperature > 38.3°C

differentials.name: Shift of WBC count to left  1
differentials.raw: Shift of WBC count to left  1

differentials.name: Abortive appendicitis = mild acute appendicitis that resolves spontaneously in an early stage of disease (after relief of inciting obstruction) in 8-15%
differentials.raw: Abortive appendicitis = mild acute appendicitis that resolves spontaneously in an early stage of disease (after relief of inciting obstruction) in 8-15%

differentials.name: Recurrent appendicitis (10-38%) = repeated similar episodic attacks of RLQ pain → appendectomy showing acute inflammation; in 70% within a year after first event
differentials.raw: Recurrent appendicitis (10-38%) = repeated similar episodic attacks of RLQ pain → appendectomy showing acute inflammation; in 70% within a year after first event

differentials.name: Chronic appendicitis (1%) = RLQ pain of > 3 weeks + no alternative diagnosis + chronic active inflammation on histology → relief of symptoms after appendectomy
differentials.raw: Chronic appendicitis (1%) = RLQ pain of > 3 weeks + no alternative diagnosis + chronic active inflammation on histology → relief of symptoms after appendectomy

differentials.name: Mortality rate of 1% (associated with perforation) Cx:   perforation (13-30-73%), abscess formation, peritonitis, wound infection, sepsis,
differentials.raw: Mortality rate of 1% (associated with perforation) Cx:   perforation (13-30-73%), abscess formation, peritonitis, wound infection, sepsis,

context: Prevalence:   > 250,000 cases annually; 1-4% in children with acute abdominal pain Lifetime risk:   7-9% in Western world population Etiology:   obstruction of appendiceal lumen by lymphoid hyperplasia (60%), fecolith (33%), foreign body (4%), stricture, tumor, parasite; Crohn disease (in 25%) Cause:   luminal obstruction from (a)  fecolith (11-52%) = hard crushable concretions from inspissation of fecal material + inorganic salts (b)  appendiceal calculus = hard noncrushable calcified stone (7-15%) (c)  lymphoid hyperplasia (d)  foreign body (e)  parasite (f)  primary tumor: carcinoid, adenoc
